original_problem: In a small town, there are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the switches on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the light bulbs. How can you figure out which switch corresponds to which light bulb?
deconstructed_problem: In a small town, there are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the switches on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the light bulbs. How can you figure out which switch corresponds to which light bulb?
original_steps: Step 1: Turn switch 1 to the 'on' position for 5 minutes to heat up the corresponding bulb.
Step 2: Turn switch 1 to the 'off' position and turn switch 2 to the 'on' position, then immediately enter the room.
Step 3: Observe the bulbs, noting which ones are on, off, and warm (but off), as the bulb corresponding to switch 1 will be warm but off.
Step 4: Identify the bulb that is currently on, which corresponds to switch 2.
Step 5: The remaining bulb, which is off and not warm, must correspond to switch 3.
skeleton_steps: ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 1: Turn switch 1 to the 'on' position for 5 minutes to heat up the corresponding bulb.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 2: Turn switch 1 to the 'off' position and turn switch 2 to the 'on' position, ତାହେଲେ (then) immediately enter the room.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 3: Observe the bulbs, noting which ones are on, off, and warm (but off), as the bulb corresponding to switch 1 will be warm but off.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 4: Identify the bulb that is currently on, which corresponds to switch 2.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 5: The remaining bulb, which is off and not warm, must correspond to switch 3.
final_answer: You can correctly identify which switch corresponds to which light bulb.
